svg.title: HNC Business Assignment Units: Business Environment PESTLE Porter Five Forces, Managing People Maslow Herzberg, Marketing 7Ps STP, Financial Management Ratio Analysis, Research Project 4500 to 6000 words with Pass Merit Distinction criteria codes
svg.text: HNC Business: Unit Framework and P/M/D Criteria
Five Core Units · Pn/Mn/Dn Criterion Codes · Research Project Mandatory · Harvard Referencing Required
CORE UNITS — Key Frameworks, Distinction Focus, and Common Criterion Failures
BUSINESS ENVIRONMENT (P5/M3/D2)
Pass: PESTLE analysis + Porter's Five Forces applied to organisation
Merit (M3): Analyse the significance of TWO macro-environmental factors
Distinction (D2): Evaluate how environmental change impacts strategic decisions
Common fail: listing PESTLE factors without analysing their strategic significance
Key sources: Johnson et al. (2017) Exploring Strategy · Porter (1980) Competitive Strategy
MANAGING PEOPLE (P4/M2/D1)
Pass: Apply Maslow + Herzberg to motivational scenario
Merit (M2): Compare motivational theories; justify recommendation for context
Distinction (D1): Critically evaluate theory limitations (Wahba & Bridwell, 1976)
Common fail: describing theories without comparing or evaluating limitations
McGregor Theory X/Y · Hersey & Blanchard Situational Leadership
MARKETING (P4/M2/D2)
Pass: Apply 7Ps marketing mix to an organisation
Merit (M2): Analyse how STP informs the marketing mix decisions
Distinction (D2): Evaluate marketing effectiveness; recommend strategic changes
Common fail: describing 7Ps without proportionate analysis across all seven elements
Segmentation / Targeting / Positioning must be connected to each mix element
FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT (P4/M2/D1)
Pass: Calculate profitability, liquidity, efficiency, gearing ratios
Merit (M2): Analyse ratio trends; compare against industry benchmarks
Distinction (D1): Evaluate financial health; recommend financing/investment strategy
Common fail: listing ratios without interpreting their financial management significance
Cash flow ≠ profit · Distinguish liquidity from solvency in analytical commentary
MANAGING A SUCCESSFUL BUSINESS PROJECT — Mandatory Unit · 4,500–6,000 words
Structure: SMART aims → Literature Review (min. 8 academic sources) → Methodology Justification → Findings → Conclusions → Recommendations
Pass: Research plan produced + primary/secondary data collected + findings presented
Merit: Methodology justified against alternatives · Literature review synthesises rather than summarises sources
Distinction: Conclusions drawn from evidence + limitations acknowledged + actionable recommendations with evidential basis
Common structural fails: aims not SMART · methodology not justified · findings and conclusions merged without distinction
DISTINCTION REQUIRES: Synthesis + Critical Evaluation + Independent Strategic Recommendation
Not "what is Porter's Five Forces" — but "to what extent does Porter's Five Forces explain the competitive dynamics of this specific industry, and what are the limitations of applying it here?"
with cited evidence for both the application and the limitation
HNC Assignment Help Service · HNC Business Unit Framework — Level 4 RQF
